The **Verizon Droid Razr** ([GSM](/wiki/GSM "GSM")/[UMTS](/wiki/Universal_Mobile_Telecommunications_System "Universal Mobile Telecommunications System") version: **Motorola Razr**; both versions styled **RAZR**)[[4]](#cite_note-4) is an [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)")-based, [4G](/wiki/4G "4G") [LTE](/wiki/Long_Term_Evolution "Long Term Evolution")-capable [smartphone](/wiki/Smartphone "Smartphone") designed by [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") that launched on [Verizon Wireless](/wiki/Verizon_Wireless "Verizon Wireless") on November 11, 2011. It was announced on October 18, 2011 in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City").

At launch, the Razr was the thinnest smartphone in the world at only 7.1 mm thick on most of the device (it does, however, have a "bump" on top that is approximately 11.1 mm thick.) and includes a 4.3-inch (110 mm) super active-matrix organic light emitting diode ([AMOLED](/wiki/AMOLED "AMOLED")) advanced [PenTile](/wiki/PenTile "PenTile")[[2]](#cite_note-a-closer-look-at-the-droid-razrs-screen-2) display, covered in a [Gorilla glass](/wiki/Gorilla_glass "Gorilla glass") screen and a [Kevlar](/wiki/Kevlar "Kevlar") back plate. It is powered by an [OMAP 4430](/wiki/Texas_Instruments_OMAP#OMAP_4 "Texas Instruments OMAP") [SoC](/wiki/System-on-chip "System-on-chip") with dual 1.2 GHz [ARM Cortex-A9](/wiki/ARM_Cortex-A9_MPCore "ARM Cortex-A9 MPCore") processor cores. Its 8-megapixel rear-facing camera can record 1080p HD videos. It comes with 1 GB of RAM and runs Android version 4.1.2.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The **Razr Maxx** is a variant with a higher capacity battery at 3300 mAh [[6]](#cite_note-DROID_RAZR_MAXX-6) an 85% increase in capacity over the 1780 mAh battery in the original RAZR. Due to the battery's larger physical size, the Razr Maxx's overall thickness is larger, at 8.99 mm.[[6]](#cite_note-DROID_RAZR_MAXX-6)

The Razr's successor, the [Droid Razr HD](/wiki/Droid_Razr_HD "Droid Razr HD"), was announced on 5 September 2012.[[7]](#cite_note-Fox_first-7)

## Availability

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Availability")]

The phone was launched in the United States on November 11, 2011, at 11:11 am.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The [GSM](/wiki/GSM "GSM")/[UMTS](/wiki/Universal_Mobile_Telecommunications_System "Universal Mobile Telecommunications System") version was launched in India on 21 November 2011 at an MRP of ₹35,000. Motorola also stated a best buy price in selected retail stores of ₹33,990 for Razr XT910.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In Australia, [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola "Motorola") launched it with the network operator [Optus](/wiki/Optus "Optus") in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), on 27 October 2011,[[11]](#cite_note-11) though not the 4G LTE variant.

In Europe it was launched first in [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria"), debuting on December 12, 2011 from VIVACOM.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Hardware

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Hardware")]

Motorola Droid Razr comes in the form factor of a slate with a [diamond-cut](/wiki/Diamond_cut "Diamond cut") [aluminum](/wiki/Aluminum "Aluminum") chassis. The display is a 4.3 inch qHD (540 × 960) Super AMOLED Advanced PenTile[2] capacitive touchscreen that is covered by scratch-resistant [Corning](/wiki/Corning_Inc. "Corning Inc.") [Gorilla Glass](/wiki/Gorilla_Glass "Gorilla Glass") with an oleophobic fingerprint-resistant coating.

The back cover is made with [DuPont](/wiki/DuPont "DuPont") [Kevlar](/wiki/Kevlar "Kevlar") fiber surrounded by a plastic structure. The internals of the Droid RAZR are packed the smartphone construction technique [Component Lamination](/w/index.php?title=Component_Lamination&action=edit&redlink=1 "Component Lamination (page does not exist)"), which physically bonds each layer of components to each other to provide a stronger body.
The whole phone is covered by an invisible layer of splash-proof nano coating, which makes the phone water-resistant to occasional liquid splashes.

The Droid Razr features four touch-capacitive buttons with the respective functions of menu, home, back and search. It uses a dual microphone system for noise reduction.

A glitch where the time of the phone resets when it powers off had been observed in about two thousand phones worldwide.

## Software

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Software")]

The phone comes installed with Android version 2.3.5 [Gingerbread](/wiki/Android_version_history#Android_2.3.x_Gingerbread "Android version history"). It uses a modified [user interface](/wiki/User_interface "User interface") called [Motoblur](/wiki/Motoblur "Motoblur") that focuses on social networking. The main features of Motoblur in the Droid Razr are:

* Unified contacts. The contacts from all services are unified and duplicates are removed. This is often a source of errors as it has troubles distinguishing between unique contacts and copies.
* Google apps. By default all the Google Apps present in the Stock Android are included, with a modified UI and with Motorola services embedded.
* [Smartactions](/w/index.php?title=Smartactions&action=edit&redlink=1 "Smartactions (page does not exist)"). A Motorola app designed to automate tasks based on a wide range of triggers such as; time, location, battery charge, Wi-Fi network, etc.
* Webtop

The Droid Razr is upgradeable to version 4.1.2 [Jelly Bean](/wiki/Android_Jelly_Bean "Android Jelly Bean"). The release of the update began on March 8, 2013 for Verizon Wireless Users. The official Over the Air (OTA) update was released soon after.[[13]](#cite_note-JB-13)

The Droid Razr had official support from the [LineageOS](/wiki/LineageOS "LineageOS") project, the latest available version being 14.1, which is based on Android 7.1.2.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Webtop

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Webtop")]

Similar to the [Motorola Atrix 4G](/wiki/Motorola_Atrix_4G "Motorola Atrix 4G"), it has the integrated "Webtop" application from Motorola. The Webtop application is launched when the phone is connected to the external display through the Laptop dock or HD multimedia dock. In Webtop mode, offering a similar user interface of a typical [Ubuntu](/wiki/Ubuntu_(operating_system) "Ubuntu (operating system)") desktop, the phone can run several applications on an external display such as the [Firefox](/wiki/Firefox "Firefox") web browser, SNS clients and 'mobile view' applications enabling total access of Atrix and its screen. In September 2011, Motorola released the source code of the Webtop application at [SourceForge](/wiki/SourceForge "SourceForge").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/79/Motorola_XT910_in_hand.jpg/330px-Motorola_XT910_in_hand.jpg)](/wiki/File:Motorola_XT910_in_hand.jpg)

Motorola Droid Razr Maxx

### Webtop 3.0

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Webtop 3.0")]

With the Ice Cream Sandwich upgrade, Webtop is now called Webtop 3.0 and no longer consists of a separate Linux OS. Instead, when the phone is connected to an external monitor via HDMI (Laptop dock or multimedia dock is no longer required) and Webtop is chosen, the phone switches to the tablet UI in ICS and is able to run all the existing applications on the phone.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Successors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Successors")]

### Razr V

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Razr V")]

The Razr V is considered a generational upgrade of the original Droid Razr. The Razr V has improvements over the original such as Android 4.0 Ice Cream Sandwich and an easier to grasp form-factor, but there are also some cost-cutting measures in order to reduce the price, since the original Droid Razr was positioned as a flagship smartphone. In Canada, the Razr V launched on [Bell Mobility](/wiki/Bell_Mobility "Bell Mobility") and [Virgin Mobile](/wiki/Virgin_Mobile "Virgin Mobile") and [Wind Mobile](/wiki/Wind_Mobile "Wind Mobile").[[1]](http://mobilesyrup.com/2012/08/19/motorola-razr-v-review/)[[2]](http://www.gsmarena.com/motorola_razr_v_xt885-5224.php)[[3]](https://web.archive.org/web/20130214062713/http://www.motorola.ca/consumers/MOTOROLA-RAZR-V/m-motorola-razr-v,en_CA,pd.html)[[4]](https://www.neowin.net/news/review-motorola-razr-v) However, the Razr V is still priced above that of a [feature phone](/wiki/Feature_phone "Feature phone") and it is not much cheaper than flagship (high-end) smartphones with better specs, such as Motorola's own Razr M and Razr HD.[[5]](http://www.techradar.com/reviews/phones/mobile-phones/motorola-razr-v-1131276/review)

## Unlocked bootloader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Unlocked bootloader")]

There is a Droid Razr variant that features the same characteristics as the Droid Razr with the difference of having an unlockable bootloader. All Razrs now can be unlocked, provided they are not the old OMAP processor.[[17]](#cite_note-17) This device, the Motorola RAZR developer edition, is currently sold in [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe").[[18]](#cite_note-Motorola-18)

The developer edition features the capability of unlocking its bootloader by a tool found in the Motorola Mobility website. An unlocked bootloader gives developers the ability to load a custom operating system onto the phone without the need to use alternative bypasses such as [Safestrap](/w/index.php?title=Safestrap&action=edit&redlink=1 "Safestrap (page does not exist)"), it also provides easy [root access](/wiki/Android_rooting "Android rooting")[[19]](#cite_note-19) without the need to use specific [root exploits](/w/index.php?title=Root_exploits&action=edit&redlink=1 "Root exploits (page does not exist)") such as the Verizon variant (XT912) and the international variant (XT910).[[20]](#cite_note-20)
